title: Complete Bibliography for Balance-Guided Oblique Trees Paper
summary: All 15 BibTeX entries successfully obtained and verified. 10 entries retrieved directly via Semantic Scholar batch API, 3 recovered via DOI/title retry, 2 manually constructed from verified web sources (Bell 2003 workshop paper, McGill 1954 classic). Multiple corrections applied: Kraskov year fixed 2003->2004, Hornung year fixed 2021->2022 and DOI corrected, Westphal year fixed 2024->2025, Breiman venue corrected, entry types standardized. Novelty gap confirmed: no 2024-2026 prior work combines spectral clustering on information-theoretic feature graphs with oblique tree construction (confirmed by dependency research_id5_it4__opus survey of 31 sources).
answer: All 15 BibTeX entries for the Balance-Guided Oblique Trees paper have been successfully obtained and verified. 10 entries were retrieved directly from Semantic Scholar's batch API in a single call [1]. Three additional entries (Lou 2013, Murthy 1994, Hornung 2022) were recovered by retrying with corrected DOIs [2, 3, 4]. Two entries (Bell 2003, McGill 1954) required manual construction from verified web sources [5, 6]. Multiple corrections were applied to Semantic Scholar output: Kraskov's year was fixed from 2003 to 2004 (matching Physical Review E vol. 69 publication date), Hornung's year was fixed from 2021 to 2022 and the DOI corrected from a university repository DOI to the publisher DOI (10.1016/j.csda.2022.107460), Westphal's year was fixed from 2024 to 2025 (AISTATS 2025 proceedings), Breiman's venue was corrected from 'Machine-mediated learning' to 'Machine Learning', and several entry types were standardized (e.g., Breiman, Hornung, McGill changed to @article). The novelty gap remains confirmed: the dependency survey (research_id5_it4__opus) examined 31 sources across spectral feature grouping, Co-Information estimation, and interpretable oblique trees, finding no prior work combining spectral clustering on an information-theoretic feature interaction graph with oblique tree split construction [7].

Citation key kept as Tan2022 for recognition (when the work was first disseminated), but year field is 2025 (publication year). All 9 authors present. PNAS DOI confirmed.

Journal name cleaned to 'Physical Review E'.

Bell (Redwood Neuroscience Institute).

queries_run: Dependency survey (research_id5_it4__opus) examined 31 sources across 4 threads
novelty_gap_check.notes: The dependency research (research_id5_it4__opus) conducted a comprehensive four-thread literature survey examining 31 sources spanning spectral feature grouping methods, Co-Information estimation bias, state-of-the-art interpretable oblique trees, and paper positioning. It confirmed a clear novelty gap: no prior work combines spectral clustering on an information-theoretic (MI/Co-Information) feature interaction graph with oblique decision tree split construction. The closest works identified were: (1) SPEC (Zhao & Liu 2007) - spectral but individual feature evaluation only; (2) GBFG (Zheng et al. 2020) - MI-based grouping but MST not spectral; (3) PIDF (Westphal et al. 2025) - PID-based synergy/redundancy but no graph/spectral/trees; (4) Interaction Forests (Hornung 2022) - tree structural interaction detection but not IT-based; (5) FoLDTree (2024) - oblique with ULDA not IT; (6) CART-ELC (2025) - exhaustive oblique search, no IT criteria; (7) Feature Graphs (BioData Mining 2025) - post-hoc spectral clustering on tree ensemble graphs, fundamentally different direction. No 2024-2026 paper was found that directly preempts the core contribution.

sources.title: Dependency research_id5_it4__opus - Spectral CoI Literature Survey
sources.summary: Four-thread survey of 31 sources confirming novelty gap: no prior work combines spectral clustering on IT feature interaction graphs with oblique tree construction

follow_up_questions: Should the FIGS citation key be updated from Tan2022 to Tan2025 to match the PNAS publication year, and does the paper draft use the ArXiv or PNAS version?
Is the SPONGE citation intended for the original AISTATS 2019 paper (ArXiv 1904.08575) or the regularized JMLR 2021 extension (ArXiv 2011.01737), and should both be cited?
Should the Westphal PIDF entry use the AISTATS proceedings DOI (when available from PMLR) instead of the ArXiv DOI currently listed?
